focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 10

By the time they got the screw free, Elise's palms were striped raw where the rope had burned through the chalk dust. The press had always looked smaller when it stood in its corner with the ink balls hanging beside it and a proof sheet pinned crooked to the wall, only another object the quarry had learned to keep alive. Once Lucien and Nadir had lifted off the frisket and unpinned the tympan, once the chase was set aside and the little drawer of furniture emptied into a flour sack, it turned ugly. The body of it had weight in the wrong places. The copper cheeks were cold with seep and old grease. It did not want to be carried.

Clemence stood over the ledgers on an upturned crate, apron tied tight over her skirt as if this were still laundry and not flight. She wet her thumb before every page turn although the lye had cracked the skin there into white seams. Her fast face moved over names, subscriptions, the book of bread tallies, the wrapper of blank labor cards and copied baptism extracts left in the quarry once the reserve forged papers went aboveground to the mourning-ribbon shop cache, the list of front addresses. She made two piles, what could be replaced and what would hang somebody if the police got their hands on it.

"That one," she said, tapping a narrow account book with a red thread down the spine. "Not because of the figures. Because Berthe writes to us in the margins when she has no other paper."

Nadir took it from her and slid it into an oilcloth bundle. He always stood a little side-on, as if rooms had taught him not to give them his whole chest. Tonight there was a folded scrap of blue handbill tucked into his sleeve, something he had started turning into one of the small paper animals he made for Lucie when the child had to sit quiet....
prev_tail_excerpt:
...t she had offered. Around her the quarry settled into its night sounds: Colombe's breath catching when the pain climbed, Lucie's brief talking in sleep, Abel still awake somewhere deeper in the passage and reciting under his breath to keep from thinking, Nadir tearing a proof sheet cleanly down the middle for tomorrow's kindling, Lucien coughing once and then smothering the rest for the sake of the others.

Elise stared into the dark until the white of the wall began to suggest itself beyond the lamp's reach. She had entered the refuge to turn lives into reportable matter. Then she had begun leaving names off pages, hiding the cache list, burning slips. Those had still been forms of observation, however compromised. This was something meaner and more binding. Beneath her hem rode bread, rent, aliases, the right to cross a checkpoint without vanishing into an office. If she failed aboveground, the loss would not be information. It would be supper. It would be the next room. It would be the thin legal fiction by which Lucie might pass for somebody the Republic had no immediate wish to lock away.

Before dawn, when the stone sweated colder and the city above had not yet started its wheels, Elise rose and dressed. Clemence was already waiting by the passage with a bundle of washing over one arm. She pressed it into Elise's hands without greeting. Trust had not grown in the night. It had only put on work clothes.

Elise took the damp weight of the laundry. Under it, under the skirt, under the discipline of her own careful face, the hidden packet pulled at her with every breath. For the first time since she had come down under the hill, she was carrying one piece of their continued existence. Then she turned toward the narrow climb to the wine cellar and the morning above it.
